examples: Opening gate routine clarified as paper-check bureaucracy rather than grain inspection so the chapter keeps chapter 6's continuity and the prose stays tactically concrete.

Corin's first explanation was tightened from clerk-summary language into speech that sounds hurried and human: 'comfort language' became 'he was being kind,' and his route report now moves like a frightened young mage talking fast.
Havel's entrance lost some explanatory drag: 'the manner of a man...' became broken-nose / tired-eyes / patience under pressure, which fits Sylvanas's one-detail-at-a-time read.
Jaina's split-scene handoff was sharpened from 'the whole building is wrong' to the plainer 'they've poisoned the whole building,' matching her audience-adjusted register.
The rejoin section was recast from clipped case-file fragments into a stronger Sylvanas thought line: 'Enough to carry back to Jaina.'
The closing no longer names the partnership in soft abstract terms; it now frames the shift as mission fact and out-of-sight reliance.
notes: Checked the gate paragraph against the style guide's demand for concrete nouns and causal scene movement.
Checked Corin's office scene for whether Sylvanas still sounded like the controlling intelligence in the room; she does.
Checked Havel's introduction for aristocratic bite versus generic stoic-soldier prose.
Checked Jaina's explanatory lines against her voice sheet's 'downshift for non-mages' rule.
Meta-audited the last two paragraphs because that was the chapter's weakest voice section.

examples: Havel's reaction line was recast from an external-snide narrator summary into something Sylvanas can actually read off his jaw and urgency.

Checked the split section for impossible access to Jaina's off-page work; the chapter keeps the solve as a report-back scene under Sylvanas POV.
Checked the argument over capture versus proof; both women stay legible through posture and spoken lines, not narrator diagnosis.
Checked the final interior shift against limited-third absolute and removed the soft abstract phrasing that had started sounding authorial.

examples: Chapter 6 established that no one was checking grain, so chapter 7 now clarifies that the gate is screening papers, not sacks.
The chapter keeps Andorhal as a living, competent city already seen from the rise; nothing is reintroduced as a fresh wonder-reveal.
The close now preserves chapter 6's relationship state: operational trust, not tenderness.

Checked plague talk against memory/world/magic.json: grain is still the shell and the host still finishes the work; only hub-packaging method is newly revealed.
Checked thread/grain-distribution-network.json: Barov remains unresolved and Andorhal remains a hub, not confirmed origin.
Checked character memory for Sylvanas: the closing interiority now matches her chapter-6 state of cold focus translated into next action.

topics: Andorhal / Strahnbrad / Dalaran / Kirin Tor during the early Plague of Lordaeron
Pre-undeath Sylvanas characterization and young Jaina characterization
findings: External lore continues to support Andorhal as a grain hub and plague distribution point, which fits the chapter's logistics emphasis.
Strahnbrad remains aligned as the visible-war pressure point that can distract Arthas from the quieter grain war.
Dalaran and the Kirin Tor fit the chapter's bureaucracy-plus-research lane; Corin's ignored reports read canon-compatible.
Pre-undeath Sylvanas remains proud, direct, tactical, and unsentimental; young Jaina remains precise, diplomatic, and intellectually fast rather than modern-scientific.
No lore change was needed; the real fixes were register and continuity, not canon.
